requirement.original: Consolidate 4 independent grant-read implementations into one canonical library (hooks/lib/allowlist.py), preserving all invariants: PreToolUse hooks read-only, posttool deletes, IS_SUBAGENT firewall intact, bypassable operations set unchanged.
requirement.clarified: Add shared internal primitives to hooks/lib/allowlist.py (_load_and_match, read_grant_for_git_command, match_grant_for_bash_command, consume_grant_for_posttool). Update pretool-git-privilege-guard.py, pretool-bash-safety.sh heredoc, and posttool-allowlist-consume.py to use them. Preserve all matching semantics exactly.
requirement.what: Move duplicated grant-file I/O + lock + JSON-load + pattern-match code out of 3 hook files into hooks/lib/allowlist.py
requirement.why: Four independent implementations create drift risk — a future change to grant file format or match semantics must be applied in four places; a miss causes a security gap in one hook but not others.

included: hooks/lib/allowlist.py — M1 read_grant_for_git_command, M2 _load_and_match internal helper, M3 consume_grant_for_posttool, S1 match_grant_for_bash_command
hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py — M4 replace _check_git_allowlist body with lib call
hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh — M5 replace heredoc grant-read section with shim calling lib
hooks/posttool-allowlist-consume.py — replace independent grant-read+unlink with consume_grant_for_posttool call
excluded: hooks/pretool-orchestrator-gate.py — already uses lib.allowlist.read_grant, no change
hooks/pretool-subagent-enforce.py — already uses lib.allowlist.read_grant, no change
hooks/pretool-aggregate-check.py — already uses lib.allowlist.read_grant, no change
hooks/userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh — grant writer, not reader, no change
IS_SUBAGENT check logic — stays in each caller, not moved to library
Grant matching semantics — must be preserved per-hook exactly as today

success_criteria: pretool-git-privilege-guard.py:_check_git_allowlist has no grant-file open/flock — delegates to lib
pretool-bash-safety.sh heredoc has no grant-file open — delegates to lib via shim
posttool-allowlist-consume.py has no os.unlink — delegates to consume_grant_for_posttool which unlinks atomically under flock
IS_SUBAGENT check in each hook still fires before any lib call
Force-push block in _evaluate_push still precedes allowlist check
Bypassable operation set unchanged: same commands pass/fail as before
constraints: PreToolUse hooks: read-only (no unlink in any lib read function)
PostToolUse: sole delete point — os.unlink only inside consume_grant_for_posttool
IS_SUBAGENT firewall: stays in each caller; lib functions are not aware of subagent context
Force-push must remain non-bypassable by generic /allow (ordering invariant in _evaluate_push)
posttool tool_name fallback path (lines 82-84) must be preserved
SIGALRM timeout in match_grant_for_bash_command (1s) must be preserved
bash heredoc stdout protocol (CONSUMED\t or renamed MATCHED\t) must remain parseable by shell case statement

path_dependent_shared_infra: hooks/lib/subagent.py — imported by posttool; no change but must remain importable
out_of_path_observed_but_not_touched: hooks/pretool-orchestrator-gate.py — already correct consumer of lib.allowlist
hooks/pretool-subagent-enforce.py — already correct consumer of lib.allowlist
hooks/pretool-aggregate-check.py — already correct consumer of lib.allowlist

root_cause_analysis.symptom: Four independent implementations of /allow grant-read logic across hooks/lib/allowlist.py, pretool-git-privilege-guard.py, pretool-bash-safety.sh, and posttool-allowlist-consume.py
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: Incremental development: lib/allowlist.py was extracted from pretool-orchestrator-gate.py in task 20260517-065529 (commit aaf3c44) but the migration was partial — git-privilege-guard got its _check_git_allowlist renamed from read_grant to _check_git_allowlist (removing dormant unlink) but not converted to use the library; bash hook and posttool were explicitly left as-is
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: aaf3c44 — refactor(hooks): extract shared lib/subagent.py and lib/allowlist.py; comment in task 20260518-094616 context.json out_of_scope_observations[0] confirms deferral
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: The task 20260517-065529 migration was scoped to remove a dangerous dormant os.unlink from git-privilege-guard and extract the library; full consolidation of bash heredoc and posttool was out of scope
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: Any future change to grant format, match semantics, or file path must be applied in 4 places; a miss in one creates a security inconsistency (one hook bypasses where another does not)
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Accumulated since 2026-05-17 task aaf3c44; actioned now by user request
observations: hooks/lib/allowlist.py:22-51 — read_grant(tool_name, sid), opens grant file, fcntl.flock(LOCK_EX), matches pattern against tool_name (inferred: static read)
hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py:141-177 — _check_git_allowlist(command, data), independent open+flock+JSON-load+match against command string, no unlink (inferred: static read)

hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:163-309 — Python heredoc with NB-flock retry + SIGALRM + compound-split; outputs CONSUMED protocol; no grant unlink (inferred: static read, comment at line 303 confirms deferred delete)
hooks/posttool-allowlist-consume.py:53-99 — open+flock+JSON-load+match (split + full + tool_name fallback) + os.unlink on match (inferred: static read)
aaf3c44 commit message confirms partial migration: 'pretool-git-privilege-guard: removed dormant os.unlink; renamed function to _check_git_allowlist' — library not yet used for command matching
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: incomplete — inferred only (static code reading). Behavioral semantics verified by code inspection; no runtime measurement taken. Sufficient for a structural refactoring task with no observable symptoms.
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: Grep for 'claude-bash-allowlist-' in hooks/ finds all 4 grant-file reference sites; static read confirms independent open+flock+match in each

git_bisect_result.bisect_blocked: not a regression — structural debt accumulation, no bisect needed

requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: _load_and_match is the single grant-file-read implementation; git guard and posttool call lib functions; no independent open+flock+JSON-load remains in hook files

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: hooks/lib/allowlist.py should be the canonical location for grant-read logic
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: grant-file open/flock/JSON-load/match lives only in lib/allowlist.py

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: IS_SUBAGENT firewall must remain intact in all 4 paths
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: each hook's subagent check fires before any lib function is called; lib functions have no subagent awareness

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: grant-consumption semantics must not change: PreToolUse hooks read-only, posttool deletes
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: consume_grant_for_posttool is the only os.unlink caller; all lib read functions have no unlink

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: scope invariant: the set of bypassable operations must be identical before and after
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: matching semantics matrix (tool_name/command/subcommand/fallback) preserved per hook as documented

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: The bash hook cannot directly call Python library functions — it can only call Python as a subprocess
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: N/A — bash hook calls Python subprocess (shim) that imports lib.allowlist

matching_semantics_matrix.description: Must be preserved exactly after consolidation

hooks.literal_match: pattern == tool_name OR pattern in tool_name

hooks.input: split subcommands + full command + tool_name fallback
hooks.literal_match: pattern == part OR pattern in part (subcommands/full); pattern == tool_name (fallback)

library_additions.purpose: Internal helper: open grant file + flock + JSON load + match against list of candidate strings
library_additions.parameters: sid: str, candidates: list[str], literal_policy: str, regex_timeout: int | None

library_additions.purpose: PreToolUse read for git guard — match pattern against full command string, substring-only literal
PreToolUse match for bash hook — compound-split + SIGALRM timeout
library_additions.parameters: command: str, sid: str, regex_timeout: int = 1

library_additions.purpose: PostToolUse atomic match+unlink — sole os.unlink site
library_additions.parameters: grant_path: Path, candidates: list[str], is_regex: bool
library_additions.atomicity: match and os.unlink happen inside single fcntl.flock(LOCK_EX) acquire

hook_changes.change: M4 — _check_git_allowlist body: remove independent open+flock+JSON+match; add import read_grant_for_git_command; call it with (command, sid). IS_SUBAGENT check and _get_session_id remain in caller.

M5 — replace lines 163-309 Python heredoc grant-read/match section with shim: sys.path.insert + import lib.allowlist.match_grant_for_bash_command + call it + print CONSUMED (or MATCHED) protocol on MatchResult. Shell wrapper, IS_SUBAGENT check, approval JSON, audit log remain unchanged.

Remove independent open+flock+JSON+match+os.unlink block (lines 53-98). Import consume_grant_for_posttool. Build candidates list (subcommands + full command + tool_name fallback) in posttool then call consume_grant_for_posttool(grant_path, candidates). Subagent check, session_id resolution remain in posttool.

component_chain.role: Canonical grant-read library — currently has read_grant only; will gain _load_and_match, read_grant_for_git_command, match_grant_for_bash_command, consume_grant_for_posttool
imports_from: stdlib: fcntl, json, re, sys, os, pathlib, signal

component_chain.role: _check_git_allowlist — independent grant-read against command string; will delegate to lib
check_and_consume_allowlist bash function — IS_SUBAGENT check + Python heredoc shim; will delegate grant-read to lib via subprocess
imports_from: hooks/lib/allowlist.py via Python subprocess shim (after M5)

component_chain.role: PostToolUse sole grant-delete hook — will delegate atomic match+unlink to lib

pre_existing_guards.purpose: Force-push/force-with-lease/delete-push remains non-bypassable even with /allow grant
IS_SUBAGENT firewall: subagents cannot consume /allow grants via git guard
IS_SUBAGENT firewall: subagents cannot consume /allow grants via bash hook
IS_SUBAGENT firewall: subagents cannot trigger grant deletion

setup.reason: non-UI — hook; cycle does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered code paths

evidence.observed: Four independent implementations of /allow grant-read logic exist across hooks/lib/allowlist.py, pretool-git-privilege-guard.py, pretool-bash-safety.sh, posttool-allowlist-consume.py

measured.value: Each of the 4 hooks independently opens /tmp/claude-bash-allowlist-<sid>.json, acquires fcntl.flock, loads JSON, and matches pattern

expected.value: Grant-file I/O, lock, JSON load, and pattern match live only in hooks/lib/allowlist.py; hook files call library functions

evidence.gap: 3 of 4 hooks have independent grant-read implementations instead of calling the library

development_approach.strategy: Extract shared primitives bottom-up: write library functions first (no external change), then update callers one at a time. Git guard (Python) is simplest; posttool (Python) is next; bash heredoc shim is last and most complex.
development_approach.validation_approach: grep-based AC verification: (1) no grant-file open in hook files, (2) no os.unlink outside lib, (3) IS_SUBAGENT checks present before lib calls, (4) force-push block precedes allowlist check in git guard, (5) existing callers (orchestrator-gate, subagent-enforce, aggregate-check) unchanged

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed 10 issues. Key findings: (1) M1+M2 only under-implements — bash heredoc and posttool can and should be consolidated; (2) named APIs preferred over generic read_grant_for_command; (3) flag-parameter approach risks breaking existing read_grant callers; (4) posttool atomicity is solvable by keeping match+unlink in a single library function under one flock; (5) bash heredoc CAN import lib.allowlist via shim while preserving stdout protocol; (6) distinct literal matching semantics per hook must be explicitly preserved; (7) IS_SUBAGENT firewall needs explicit ACs; (8) CONSUMED token in bash hook is stale; (9) force-push ordering invariant must be documented; (10) consolidation verdict must cover all 4 hooks.

findings.issue: M1+M2 only under-implements the user need — bash and posttool still have independent grant parsing
findings.proposed_fix: Centralize shared primitives for all 4 hooks in lib/allowlist.py
findings.rationale: Applied: spec now includes M3 (consume_grant_for_posttool), M5 (bash shim), S1 (match_grant_for_bash_command)

findings.issue: read_grant_for_command() is too generic — git/bash/posttool matching semantics differ
findings.proposed_fix: Use named APIs: read_grant_for_git_command, match_grant_for_bash_command, consume_grant_for_posttool
findings.rationale: Applied: spec uses distinct named functions per hook context

findings.issue: Boolean/flag parameter on read_grant() risks changing existing tool-name match behavior
findings.proposed_fix: Keep read_grant backward-compatible; use private _load_and_match helper
findings.rationale: Applied: M2 defines _load_and_match as private helper; read_grant signature unchanged

findings.issue: Posttool atomicity concern is valid but solvable — move match+unlink into lib under same flock
findings.proposed_fix: Add consume_grant_for_posttool that does match and unlink under single flock
findings.rationale: Applied: M3 specifies consume_grant_for_posttool with atomicity requirement (AC5)

findings.issue: Bash hook is not truly unconsolidatable — heredoc can import lib.allowlist as a shim
findings.proposed_fix: Replace heredoc grant-read section with shim calling match_grant_for_bash_command
findings.rationale: Applied: M5 defines bash heredoc shim approach; stdout protocol preserved

findings.issue: Distinct literal matching semantics per hook not explicitly preserved in spec
findings.proposed_fix: Add acceptance matrix requiring each hook's current match semantics to be unchanged
findings.rationale: Applied: matching_semantics_matrix in context + table in spec; each lib function has named literal_policy

findings.issue: IS_SUBAGENT firewall stated but not acceptance-tested
findings.proposed_fix: Add explicit AC: for payloads with agent_id, no grant matches and posttool does not delete
findings.rationale: Applied: AC8 covers all three hook-specific IS_SUBAGENT paths

findings.issue: Bash hook CONSUMED token is stale — comments say deletion deferred but token says CONSUMED
findings.proposed_fix: Rename to MATCHED internally if no external parser depends on it
findings.rationale: Applied: Could Have item in spec. Token is only consumed by local case statement — rename is safe

findings.issue: Spec omits git push ordering invariant — force-push block must precede allowlist check
findings.proposed_fix: Add acceptance wording that ref-mutation/force-push block remains non-bypassable
findings.rationale: Applied: AC3 + pre_existing_guards[0] + Technical Hints note

findings.issue: CONSOLIDATION VERDICT M1+M2 only is the main precision failure
findings.proposed_fix: Centralize all shared primitives; update all 4 hooks
findings.rationale: Applied: spec now covers all 4 hooks with M1-M5

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 10 Codex findings applied. M3/M5/S1 added to expand from M1+M2 to full consolidation. Named API functions replace generic name. _load_and_match private helper preserves backward compatibility. consume_grant_for_posttool preserves atomicity. IS_SUBAGENT firewall AC added. Force-push ordering AC added. Matching semantics matrix added.
